summary: Gruber notes that Apple has announced April 29 as the ship date for Mac OS X 10.4 Tiger, and Amazon now offers the family pack 5-user version for pre-order alongside the single-user version. He highlights a $50 rebate form available but not prominently advertised on the family pack product page. The post serves as both a news brief and an affiliate pitch, encouraging readers to purchase through his Amazon links to support Daring Fireball.
key_points: Apple set April 29, 2005 as the official Tiger release date
Amazon made the 5-user family pack available for pre-order alongside the single-user version
A $50 rebate form exists but is not mentioned on the family pack product page, only linked from the single-user page
Gruber promotes purchasing through his Amazon affiliate links to support the site
money_quotes: Amazon's product page for the family pack doesn't mention it, but the rebate form here can save you $50.
Buying the Tiger family pack via the links at the top of this page will both save you some money and help support Daring Fireball via Amazon's filthy associates lucre.
key_insight: A brief consumer tip pairing Tiger pre-order news with an easy affiliate pitch, delivered with Gruber's characteristic self-aware candor about monetization.
